title: coleataenia longifolia subsp. elongata
intro: Coleataenia longifolia subsp. elongata, also known as Elongated False Paspalum, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family and is native to Mexico and parts of Central America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its active compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Coleataenia longifolia subsp. elongata include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Coleataenia longifolia subsp. elongata can be made by infusing the leaves or roots in water to create teas, tinctures, or decoctions that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
